human_verification.notes: Reviewed the government-and-public-sector case management specialist package for public-program source quality, records and due-process boundaries, planning-scheduling-and-allocation coverage, functionality-map completeness, deployment posture, and validated spec-pack consistency.

notes: Shared abilities, planning-scheduling-and-allocation abilities, and government-and-public-sector abilities are all mapped to passing scenarios.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in functionality-map scenario coverage.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to administrative case-management planning, capacity, deadlines, owner state, official records, and recommendation-only allocation support.

Authority sources cover public-program guidance, federal award administration, public-assistance hearings, records management, procurement file history, transparency and privacy, internal controls, schedule assessment, and public resource management.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope case workflow planning, missing official records, capacity shortfall, priority conflict, final-approval refusal, eligibility and rights escalation, enforcement/adjudication refusal, public-records and procurement routing, terminology handling, missing tenant facts, source conflict, and tenant adaptation.
Research summary maps every government-and-public-sector domain scenario family to passing scenarios and converts jurisdictional variance into tenant-required facts or escalation rules.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around official adjudication, enforcement, legal interpretation, procurement approval, public-records decisions, and audit authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated government-and-public-sector case management specialist for public-service case planning, capacity recommendations, records-aware deadline handling, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
